tax: Placed at family rank, Vitric Magmatics is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a cooled volcanic-glass body. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a glass body with its own quench origin.
ap: The grouping is recognised by an angular black-glass figure. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a litter of glass flakes and cut-marks — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Vitric Magmatics occupies quenched lava margins and obsidian flows. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a fast quench freezes lava to glass and recur with a fresh quench re-forms the glass, sustaining themselves on residual rock-heat.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: stalks and slashes while warm, shatters under a hard blow. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: glass body and razor edges. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: cutting and shrapnel-burst. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Vitric Magmatics matters because cuts and scatters shrapnel on the obsidian margins. It lets survey reports sort cooled-glass hazards from molten ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Vitric Magmatics is the family-rank grouping of cooled-glass fire elementals of the volcanic-glass kind within the True Elementals. Its members share a brittle volcanic-glass body that cuts and shatters into shrapnel, and divide below into genus groups by glass type and residual heat. What a molten elemental becomes when chilled fast.
